verses:
viśuddha vairāgī kare nāma⋅saṅkīrtana
māgiyā khāiyā kare jīvana⋅jāpana

vairāgī haiyā jevā kare parāpekṣā
kāryasiddhi nahe, kṛṣṇa karena upekṣā

vairāgī haiyā kare jihvāra lālasa
paramārtha jāya, āra haya rasera vaśa

vairāgī karibe sadā nāma⋅saṅkīrtana
śāka⋅patra⋅phala⋅mūle udara bharaṇa

jihvāra lālase jei samāje veḍāya
śiśnodara⋅parāyaṇa kṛṣṇa nāhi pāya

vairāgī bhāi grāmyakathā nā śunibe kāṇe
grāmyavārtā nā kahibe jave milibe āne

svapaneo nā kara bhāi strī⋅sambhāṣaṇa
gṛhe strī chāḍiyā bhāi āsiyācha vana

jadi cāha praṇaya rākhite gaurāṅgera sane
choṭa haridāsera kathā thāke jena mane

bhāla nā khāibe, āra bhāla nā paribe
hṛdayete rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa sarvadā sevibe

baḍa hari⋅dāsera nyāya kṛṣṇa⋅nāma balibe vadane
aṣṭa⋅kāla rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa sevibe kuñja⋅vane
en_translation:
A pure renunciant performs the congregational chanting of the Holy Names. He maintains his life only by eating what he receives through begging.

If a renunciant depends on others for his needs, his purpose is not achieved, and Lord Kṛṣṇa neglects him.

If a renunciant indulges the desires of his tongue, his spiritual goal is lost, and he becomes a slave to material tastes.

A renunciant should always perform the congregational chanting. He should fill his belly only with vegetables, leaves, fruits, and roots.

One who wanders through society just to satisfy the desires of the tongue, being dedicated only to his stomach and genitals, will never attain Lord Kṛṣṇa.

O renunciant brother! Do not let mundane talk enter your ears. Do not speak of worldly news when you meet others.

O brother! Do not even converse with women in your dreams. Remember, you have left your wife at home to come to the forest of renunciation.

If you wish to maintain your love for Lord Gaurāṅga, then always keep the story of Choṭa Haridāsa in your mind.

Do not eat palatable foods, and do not wear fine clothes. Always serve Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa within your heart.

Like the senior Haridāsa Ṭhākura, always chant the Holy Names of Kṛṣṇa. During all eight periods of the day, serve Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in the groves of Vṛndāvana.
